title: Hieracium subulatidens
taxonomy: Hieracium subulatidens is a species within the genus Hieracium, which is a part of the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is classified as a member of the subgenus Pilosella. The species is further classified as part of the Pilosella subgenus, which is a group of plants with a distinctive pilose (hairy) appearance. At the family level, it is classified as a member of the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. In the broader sense, Hieracium subulatidens is classified within the kingdom Plantae, making it a flowering plant.
preparations: Hieracium subulatidens has been used in traditional herbal medicine, with preparations often made from its leaves and stems. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may be used to soothe skin irritations. In some regions, the plant is used to make teas or infusions, believed to have a calming effect on the body. However, more research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits and risks associated with using Hieracium subulatidens. Caution should be exercised when handling the plant, as it may cause skin irritation in some individuals.
